data.title: Dogecoin rises 14% in a week, worth $39 billion with 2% increase in last 24 hours. Futures market grows by 19% in a week.
data.description: DOGECOIN, a popular cryptocurrency, saw an increase in value as investors showed a greater appetite for risk. The meme coin, with a market capitalization of $39 billion, rose by 2% in the past day, following the upward trend of other major cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum. Over the week, DOGE surged by over 14%. This increase sparked activity in the futures market, with open interest in DOGE futures spiking by 8.14% in the last 24 hours and 19% over the week, according to Coinglass. Notably, more than 75% of Binance traders holding DOGE positions were betting on its price to rise.

data.title: BNB reaches $1,111 peak with strong user metrics. Whales and treasuries boost, token burns drive price.
data.description: In July, industry experts disclosed to a media outlet that large entities and corporate treasuries accumulating BNB, paired with routine token burns, were propelling the coin's value upwards.

data.title: All money to be stablecoins by 2030 per Tether co-founder Reeve Collins; dollars, euros on blockchain in 5 years.
data.description: Reeve Collins, co-founder of Tether, predicted that various currencies, such as dollars and euros, will probably be integrated into the blockchain system in the next five years.

data.title: US SEC delays decision on Canary Litecoin ETF during government shutdown, causing uncertainty.
data.description: The decision deadline for the Canary Litecoin ETF by the US SEC has passed without resolution, contributing to uncertainty. This delay comes amidst a government shutdown and the introduction of new generic listing standards. The SEC's delay in reaching a decision has left investors and market participants in a state of flux.

data.title: Fidelity warns of potential 1999-style market melt-up due to AI-driven boom, drawing parallels to Dot-Com Bubble. Fed rate cuts and current market dynamics cited. Bubble speculations grow. Warren Buffet Ratio tops 216%, valuations mimic 1999 crash.@api_NLP_querysetting虽然我。。.
data.description: Jurrien Timmer, Director of Global Macro at Fidelity Investments, warns of a potential market bubble similar to the dot-com era in the late 1990s. He points out that the current AI-driven market boom resembles the trajectory of the 1994-2000 soft landing bull market, particularly citing similarities in the past six months (April-October 2025) to the 1998-2000 post-LTCM melt-up. Timmer highlights the role of monetary policy, mentioning three rate cuts from former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan during the earlier boom and suggesting a potential similar scenario in current policy. Concerns arise regarding the possibility of more companies getting caught up in the AI mania, leading to a market melt-up. Timmer's observations coincide with rising worries about market bubbles and overvaluation, with Warren Buffett Ratio exceeding 216% and valuation metrics echoing the 1999 crash. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Fingerprint Cards AB completes IP deal with PixArt Imaging Inc. for SEK 19 million upfront plus royalties potential.
data.description: Today, Fingerprint Cards AB (FPC) has reported its third IP monetization deal in less than a year, partnering with PixArt Imaging Inc. The agreement involves the transfer of specific fingerprint sensor technology assets and licensing of certain patents and algorithm software for PC applications. FPC is set to receive an upfront payment of USD 2.0 million (about SEK 19 million) and will also be eligible for royalties on future products created by PixArt using the licensed technology. The payment is scheduled for the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: Karolinska Development's Dilafor obtains US patent for tafoxiparin in labor priming; key asset for phase 3 development.
data.description: In Stockholm, Sweden on October 3, 2025, Karolinska Development AB revealed that its portfolio company Dilafor has secured a patent in the US safeguarding its drug candidate tafoxiparin for the primary indication of priming labor. The patent is crucial as Dilafor progresses into the phase 3 clinical trials of tafoxiparin. Karolinska Development AB is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm under the symbol KDEV.

data.title: Solana price targets $253 resistance after breaking key moving averages, trading at $231.42 with a 3.17% increase.
data.description: The cryptocurrency SOL is currently trading at $231.42, showing a 3.17% increase. Bullish investors are aiming for a $253 resistance level after a clear breakthrough above the 20 and 50-day moving averages, supported by strong momentum indicators.
